audit_items.proof_trace: All public API entry points check tpool/pool for NULL before use, all allocation results are verified before dereference, and the one theoretical gap (task->ltt_start_routine called at L1053 without a null guard) is defended by the invariant that submit2 callers always pass valid function pointers — no LDAP protocol path can supply a NULL start_routine. Internal functions like ldap_pvt_thread_pool_tid and context_reset lack null guards on their context argument but are only called with non-NULL contexts from pool_wrapper (L1064) or callers using pool_context() which returns a static fallback and never NULL.
